question: Explain the basic concept of text steganography and describe two simple techniques used to hide data within a text file.
expectedPoints: Definition: Text steganography is the practice of concealing a secret message within a carrier text file.
Technique 1: Using whitespace characters (spaces, tabs) at the end of lines to encode binary data.
Technique 2: Using synonyms or linguistically similar words to represent binary values (e.g., 'big' for 1, 'large' for 0).

question: Describe the format-based method of text steganography and analyze one major advantage and one significant limitation of this approach compared to other data hiding techniques.
expectedPoints: Method Description: Hiding data by exploiting the formatting features of a text document (e.g., slight changes to font size, color, or type that are imperceptible to a casual reader).
Advantage: Can achieve a relatively high embedding capacity without altering the visible semantic meaning of the text.
Limitation: The hidden data is often fragile and can be easily destroyed by copying and pasting the text into a plain text format or by reformatting the document.

question: Identify the primary goal of data hiding in the context of text-based steganography.
expectedPoints: The primary goal is to achieve covert communication by embedding a secret message within an ordinary, innocent-looking text carrier so that the very existence of the message is concealed from an unintended observer.
